assertion.name: Soluble Aβ dimers isolated directly from human Alzheimer's brain
assertion.statement: Soluble amyloid-β dimers extracted directly from the cerebral cortex of human subjects with Alzheimer's disease (Shankar et al. 2008), shown to impair synaptic plasticity and memory.
assertion.note: A genuinely DISJOINT upstream from Aβ*56: a different species (dimers, not the 56-kDa assembly), a different laboratory (Selkoe/Walsh, Harvard/Dublin), a different preparation (direct human-brain extraction, not Tg2576 mice), and no derivation from the Lesné 2006 characterization. Independence on the provenance dimension really does hold between this and ent-abeta56 — which is why the contrast COMBINES.
